availability.summary: Live probes returned a 200 download job response for a bounded request and clear 400 validation errors for empty filters and unsupported file_format.

sources.title: USAspending row-limited award download view and validation
RT1 reviewer evidence for award download columns

parameters.description: Search filters that define which prime award summaries and subawards are exported.

documented.notes: The contract defines many nested filter structures shared with search.

observed.notes: An empty object returned HTTP 400 with 'At least one filter is required.'

constraints: Must include at least one filter key.

parameters.description: Keyword filter used to select awards for export.
constraints: Keyword-only downloads can be large unless combined with limit or other filters.

parameters.description: Optional action-date or modified-date filter for the exported award population.

observed.notes: Not live-probed on 2026-05-09 in this bundle; current raw profile has prior observations.

constraints: Each object should include start_date and end_date.

parameters.description: Optional award type filter. When omitted, the live service injects a comprehensive default award_type_codes list into download_request.filters.

observed.defaultBehavior: Omitted award_type_codes were injected in the response, including procurement, assistance, IDV, '-1', and F001-F010 values.

constraints: Do not assume the default award type code list is static.

parameters.description: Optional list of export column identifiers. Curated starter crosswalk: award_id_piid emits CSV header award_id_piid and represents the PIID-style contract award identifier (closest search label Award ID); recipient_name emits recipient_name and represents the recipient legal/business name (closest search label Recipient Name).

observed.notes: The live response echoed requested columns in download_request, and reviewer ZIP inspection reported a header row matching award_id_piid and recipient_name exactly.

constraints: For the reviewed starter pair, generated CSV headers matched the requested column IDs exactly: award_id_piid and recipient_name.
Curated starter meanings: award_id_piid is the PIID-style contract award identifier; recipient_name is the recipient legal/business name.
Do not assume award_id_piid is the only award identifier across award types; source-defined mappings also expose award_id_fain and award_id_uri.
Column validation is weaker for this endpoint than for account downloads; prefer known USAspending column IDs.

parameters.description: File format for the generated files inside the ZIP.

observed.defaultBehavior: Omitted file_format defaults to csv; explicit csv was echoed in the live response.

constraints: Only csv, tsv, and pstxt are valid.

parameters.description: Maximum rows to include in generated files.

observed.defaultBehavior: Current profile and source indicate a default maximum download limit; a live request with limit 1 was accepted and echoed.

constraints: Use small limits for probes; large limits create larger asynchronous jobs.

templates.name: small_keyword_award_download
templates.description: Start a small CSV award download job for a keyword search and two explicit starter columns that can be parsed by header name.

templates.name: poll_download_status
templates.description: After a successful response, call the returned status_url or v2__download__status with file_name to check generation status.

response.shapeSummary: A download job descriptor, not the exported rows. The response contains status_url, file_name, file_url, and the normalized download_request used by the backend.

fields.description: URL for polling the generated ZIP status.
constraints: Use the file_name query value to call the status endpoint through MCP when possible.

fields.description: URL for the generated ZIP file once ready.
Normalized request that will be processed by the download backend, including server-injected defaults.

observed.notes: Live response included columns, download_types, file_format, filters, limit, and request_type keys.

constraints: This object is more reliable for effective defaults than the original request body.

notes: The raw profile exposes filters as an opaque object; the semantic bundle records important nested filter behavior and defaults.
The semantic bundle marks this endpoint as asynchronous and externally side-effecting because it creates a download job.
This repair adds an evidence-backed starter crosswalk for award_id_piid and recipient_name, but the full award download column catalog is still not enumerated.

quirks.statement: A successful call starts or retrieves a download job; it does not return award rows.
quirks.impact: Agents must poll status_url or use the download status endpoint before assuming the file is ready.

quirks.statement: The live service injects default award_type_codes when omitted, including F001-F010 values observed on 2026-05-09.
quirks.impact: Consumers should use download_request.filters as the effective request and should not freeze an older default code list.

quirks.statement: For the reviewed starter pair, generated CSV headers matched the raw requested column IDs award_id_piid and recipient_name.
quirks.impact: Parser generation can bind directly to those header names without a second rename step.

gaps.statement: This bundle still does not enumerate every valid export column, but it now catalogs a small evidence-backed starter crosswalk for award_id_piid and recipient_name.
gaps.impact: Use the starter crosswalk for safe parser bootstrapping; verify additional or obscure column IDs before treating them as stable schema.

risks.statement: Large or broad downloads create backend jobs and may be expensive or slow.
risks.impact: Probe with small limit values and narrow filters; do not run broad downloads as casual validation.
